understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to move selectable pieces to goal positions marked with '+' on the grid. The level required coordinating two selectable pieces at [3, 24] and [18, 21] to reach their respective goal positions, likely by pushing blocks or navigating around walls.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The game uses a pixel-based coordinate system (3 pixels per cell). Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) shift the selected piece in that direction. CLICK with a target_position switches control between multiple selectable pieces. Pushable blocks shift along with the selected piece if adjacent in the movement direction. Walls block movement and have varying behaviors that must be discovered through interaction. The level has a step budget (started at 50 steps) that decrements with each action. I exhausted the budget by repeatedly switching between the two selectables without making progress toward the goals, suggesting I failed to understand the required solution path or mismanaged the movement strategy needed to position the pieces correctly.
